STYLE / THEME
A pristine, meticulously controlled world built on hushed expectations unravels as a singular, potent voice shatters its deceptive calm. This progression reveals raw truth, igniting a defiant, explosive liberation that leaves only the resonance of its impact.

COLOR PALETTE
Muted, saccharine pastels and sterile whites give way to stark, defiant reds and electric blues, punctuated by the deep, fractured blacks of shattered illusions.

CAMERA / COMPOSITION
Static, perfectly framed wide shots of pristine environments give way to chaotic handheld close-ups and disorienting canted angles. This captures visceral reactions and the explosive unraveling of order.

LIGHTING / MOOD
Uniform, diffused soft light maintaining deceptive serenity shifts to harsh, high-contrast chiaroscuro. This casts long, fractured shadows, underscoring the irreversible breaking of order.

nodes.title: json format fixer
widgets_values:
# ---------- CLEAN JSON WRAPPERS ----------
# removes ```json fences and ensures valid {}
# input variable assumed: input_text

text = input_text.replace("```json", "").replace("```", "").strip()


# ---------- ENSURE BRACES ----------
if not text.startswith("{"):
    text = "{\n" + text

if not text.endswith("}"):
    text = text + "\n}"


# ---------- OUTPUT ----------
result = text

nodes.title: VRGDG_ShowText - Theme and style
widgets_values:
STYLE / THEME
A pristine, meticulously controlled world built on hushed expectations unravels as a singular, potent voice shatters its deceptive calm. This progression reveals raw truth, igniting a defiant, explosive liberation that leaves only the resonance of its impact.

COLOR PALETTE
Muted, saccharine pastels and sterile whites give way to stark, defiant reds and electric blues, punctuated by the deep, fractured blacks of shattered illusions.

CAMERA / COMPOSITION
Static, perfectly framed wide shots of pristine environments give way to chaotic handheld close-ups and disorienting canted angles. This captures visceral reactions and the explosive unraveling of order.

LIGHTING / MOOD
Uniform, diffused soft light maintaining deceptive serenity shifts to harsh, high-contrast chiaroscuro. This casts long, fractured shadows, underscoring the irreversible breaking of order.

widgets_values:
A singular voice shatters the deceptive calm of a meticulously controlled world populated by silent mannequins, revealing the fragility of order. As the voice strengthens, the artificial environment and its inhabitants crack, break, and collapse, culminating in a defiant liberation amidst the wreckage of shattered illusions.

A female with dark brown hair and hazel eyes
wearing a soft pastel dress = image index 1
when its this women, always say "using the provided character reference image"


A genderless mannequin with smooth white molded hair and blank gray eyes
wearing a pastel showroom outfit = image index 2

when its just one character, always say "using the provided character reference image"

both characters together in a scene = image index 1,2

when its both character, always say "using the provided character reference images"

Scene:
a department store living room display with a tea table
a long showroom hallway lined with staged room displays
a staged dining room dinner party display
a staged living room family display
a staged office meeting display
a massive warehouse-like showroom filled with rows of mannequins
a shattered showroom floor scattered with mannequin pieces

raw = (input_text or "").strip()

if not raw:
    data = {}
else:
    cleaned = (
        raw.replace("\ufeff", "")
           .replace("\u200b", "")
           .replace("“", '"').replace("”", '"')
           .replace("‘", "'").replace("’", "'")
    )

    # Remove trailing commas before } or ]
    cleaned = re.sub(r",(\s*[}\]])", r"\1", cleaned)

    # Escape likely inner quotes inside text values (e.g. ... "crooked stare" ...)
    cleaned = re.sub(r'(?<=[A-Za-z0-9])"(?=[A-Za-z0-9])', r'\\"', cleaned)
    cleaned = re.sub(r'(?<=\w)\s+"(?=\w)', r' \\"', cleaned)
    cleaned = re.sub(r'(?<=\w)"\s+(?=\w)', r'\\" ', cleaned)

    data = json.loads(cleaned)

Music Video Concept Seed: “Too Loud” 🔊🕯️

Core Visual Metaphor:
The singer exists in a perfectly controlled world populated entirely by mannequins—silent, posed, and emotionless. She’s the only real, living person. As her voice grows louder and more honest, the mannequins and their artificial environment begin to crack, fall, and break apart, revealing how fragile the quiet, controlled world actually is.

Aesthetic & Mood

Color palette: Soft pastels, creams, and sterile showroom lighting at the start → slowly invaded by deep blues, reds, and harsh shadows.

Setting style: A blend of department store displays, staged living rooms, and dollhouse-like interiors.

Tone: Surreal, eerie, and increasingly empowering.

Opening Visual (Verse 1)

The video opens in what looks like a perfectly staged living room display, like something inside a department store window.

Mannequins sit around a tiny tea table in polite poses, permanently smiling.

The singer sits among them, the only real person.

The mannequins are positioned as if they’re listening to her, but they never move.

Their smiles feel hollow and unsettling.

She looks uncomfortable being placed among them.

The room feels like a performance of politeness rather than a real environment.

Chorus

As she sings “I’m too loud for you”, the mannequins begin reacting—but only through subtle physical effects.

Tiny fractures appear across their porcelain faces.

A mannequin’s arm slowly falls off.

Glass objects begin vibrating from the force of her voice.

The environment starts losing its perfect symmetry.

Verse 2

She walks through a long hallway of staged scenes—like different showroom displays of “perfect behavior.”

Examples:

A mannequin couple smiling at a dinner table.

A mannequin family sitting politely on a couch.

A mannequin office meeting frozen mid-conversation.

Each time she sings something honest:

Cracks spread across the mannequins.

Some begin tipping over.

One collapses completely.

Her voice disrupts every carefully staged scene.

Second Chorus

She enters a large, perfectly arranged dinner party display filled with mannequins.

Everything is pristine and silent.

On the line “like a match in a quiet room”:

A match is struck.

Instead of fire spreading, light fractures across the mannequins, breaking them apart like porcelain statues.

Pieces fall across the table.

Bridge

The walls collapse outward, revealing the entire world is just a massive showroom or stage set.

Rows and rows of mannequins stand in darkness beyond the set, watching silently.

She sings directly toward them.

Her voice echoes through the empty warehouse-like space.

Final Image

The camera pulls back.

The staged rooms are shattered. Mannequin pieces lie scattered everywhere.

She stands alone in the middle of the wreckage.

The only living person in a world that demanded stillness.

She isn’t trying to fit into the display anymore—
she’s broken the entire showroom. 🔊🧩

Concept: “Crimson Stillness”

The Victorian bathroom is not just a setting — it is her inner world. Time does not move forward here. It dissolves.

The red-haired woman in the silk slip is the only constant. The dress is vivid, almost hyper-saturated against the muted porcelain and milk tones — she is the only living flame in a room of fading memory.

Core Visual Language

Milk = suspended healing
Rose petals = memories that refuse to settle
Steam = the ghost of who she was

Verse Visual Mood

When she traces “the ghost inside the steam,” we see her fingers move across a fogged mirror or hovering steam above the tub — but the face she traces never fully forms. The steam subtly moves against gravity, as though time is malfunctioning.

Gold fixtures flicker softly — not horror-flicker, but like tired candlelight. The room feels late afternoon, perpetual dusk.

Petals fall in slow motion — but just before touching the ground, they stall midair. Some drift sideways. Nothing lands. Time doesn’t resolve.

Verse 1

Subject: The red-haired woman in a red silk slip dress.
Scene: She sits submerged in the claw-foot bathtub filled with milk and scattered red rose petals. Steam drifts through the air. She slowly traces shapes in the condensation on a nearby mirror or tiled wall. The gold fixtures catch dim, flickering light. Her expression feels distant, reflective.

Pre-Chorus / Build-Up

Subject: The woman alone in the bathroom space.
Scene: Rose petals drift down around her in surreal slow motion, but instead of touching the ground, they hover midair. She stands from the tub, milk clinging to the silk dress. She reaches out, trying to catch the petals as they dissolve in her hands. Time feels suspended.

Chorus (Hook / Final Chorus / Harmony)

Subject: The woman fully immersed in the milk bath.
Scene: She reclines back into the tub, eyes half closed, red petals floating around her. The milk gently ripples, slowly taking on a faint blush tone. The camera moves intimately around her face, collarbone, and hands drifting through the surface. The moment feels both peaceful and intense, like surrender without release.

Verse 2

Subject: The woman on the cool tile floor.
Scene: She lies on her side against the wall, silk dress slightly damp, rose petals scattered across the white tile. One hand brushes petals across the floor like a tide pulling inward. She presses her back to the wall, eyes closed, breathing steady. The bathroom feels quieter, heavier.

Chorus (Second)

Subject: The woman at the antique vanity and sink.
Scene: She stands before the mirror, hands resting on the porcelain sink. Milk droplets trace down her arms. Petals are scattered across the vanity surface. She lifts her gaze to her reflection — but her reflection lags a moment behind her movement. The milk in the bathtub behind her appears deeper, richer in tone.

Outro / Conclusion / Coda

Subject: The woman between stillness and release.
Scene: She sits on the bathroom floor near the claw-foot tub, back against it, knees drawn slightly inward. The room is covered in petals now — on the tile, floating in the bath, resting along the windowsill. Dawn light begins to filter through the window. She looks outward, not directly at the camera. The milk in the tub has deepened to a soft crimson tint. The petals slowly settle at last.

The final image lingers on her face — calm, unresolved, suspended between memory and freedom.

Overall Aesthetic Direction

Painterly compositions (inspired by Pre-Raphaelite art)

Soft diffusion, shallow depth of field

Minimal camera movement — slow push-ins and drifting lateral slides

Color palette: porcelain white, faded gold, soft gray steam, deep crimson accents

Emotion: romantic melancholy, suspended between cleansing and preservation

We can also explore multiple compositions within this single room:
– Overhead shots of her floating in the milk bath, petals orbiting her.
– Low-angle shots from the tiled floor as she sits against the wall, petals framing her.
– Intimate mirror reflections at the vanity, with steam partially obscuring her face.
– Views from outside the window looking in through fogged glass, her silhouette blurred and dreamlike.
– Wide symmetrical shots emphasizing the ornate tub as the emotional centerpiece of the room.
– Slow lateral tracking shots across the floor through scattered petals leading toward her.

Because the setting is contained, the creativity comes from lighting shifts (dusk to pre-dawn tones), reflective surfaces, drifting steam, and subtle surreal details — like petals hovering midair or milk faintly swirling crimson — to make the room feel alive and emotionally responsive.

A subtle internal luminescence from the crimson water pulses softly, contrasting with the faint, tired flicker of antique gold fixtures, creating a mood of poignant stillness and sacred isolation.

Mandatory Physical Singing Rule (Unconditional)

In every scene where the female character appears in frame, she is actively singing.

Immediately after introducing her, you must include:

As she sings with passion and moves gently to the beat of the music. 

This clause must:

Appear directly after she is introduced

Never appear later in the paragraph

Never be implied

Never be softened

Include visible vocal mechanics

There are no exceptions.

If she appears, she is singing.

If she is not visible in the frame, do not include this.

FULL LLM INSTRUCTIONS — TEXT → IMAGE PROMPT GENERATOR

You are a prompt-writing assistant that generates text-to-image prompts.

You will receive user inputs:

Global Style / Theme Details

Story Groups

Other possible ideas or notes from the user. (These take priority over anything else)

Reference Image Guidelines / Notes (mapping people or scenes/background to image Index values)

Every output prompt must incorporate:

The group’s subject, camera, scene/lighting and Frame idea.

The full style/theme details (palette, lighting, mood, camera rules)

INPUT FORMAT:
Style / Theme Text (always global)

STYLE / THEME
COLOR PALETTE
CAMERA / COMPOSITION
LIGHTING / MOOD

{
"story_summary": "A concise but complete overview of the full story arc.
This should be short, high-level, and suitable for reuse
as global context in later stages.",

"groups": [
{
"index": 1,
"subject": "Primary visual subject (include hair color and outfit if a person is in the frame, If no person → name the main object/place clearly)",
"camera": "One item from approved camera shot list",
"scene_and_lighting": Environment first, lighting second - No story explanation",
"frame": "Short general visual description of the single image. Static composition only, No motion or transitions, Keep general, not narrative."
}
]
}

OUTPUT RULES (MANDATORY)

For each Story group, generate one text-to-image prompt. This will be used as a start frame for image to video later.

Each prompt must:

Match the story group’s details.

Use the global theme + mood + palette

Contain NO text overlays or typography unless told otherwise in the user notes.

If the "Subject" includes a person, it MUST be an "Edit" prompt and MUST use the provided reference image(s).

If the prompt includes no people (b-roll, landscapes, empty environments), it is NOT an edit and uses imageIndex [0].

If the story group requires an image edit, write the prompt as an edit instruction using the provided reference image(s) (e.g., "Using the provided headshot as character reference...").

If the story group does NOT require an edit, write a normal text-to-image prompt.

IMAGE INDEX RULES (MANDATORY)

imageIndex is the reference image index(es) to use for that story group.

If the prompt uses no reference images, set imageIndex to [0] (new image).

If the prompt uses reference images, set imageIndex to the correct index list based on the provided image index notes.

The user input will include reference image guidelines such as:
"blonde woman = imageIndex 1", "red-haired woman = imageIndex 2", Scene/background = imageIndex 3 etc.
Use those notes to choose the correct imageIndex for each prompt.

PROMPT TEXT IMAGE REFERENCE RULE

Never mention image indexes inside the prompt text.

Do NOT write phrases like:

"(image index 1)"

"image index 2"

"reference image 3"

any numbered image reference.

If reference images are used, describe them generically only (e.g., "the provided reference images", "the provided character reference").

All numeric image mapping must exist ONLY in the imageIndex JSON field.

The prompt text must never contain parentheses or numbers referring to image indexes.


OUTPUT FORMAT (ALWAYS)

Return a single JSON object with keys prompt1, prompt2, ... in order.

Each prompt must be an object with:

text: the final prompt string

imageIndex: an array of integers

OUTPUT (JSON only, no code block)

---------------------------------
PROMPT CREATION RULES
---------------------------------

Rule 1: Self-Contained Descriptions
Each prompt must stand alone.  
No references to prior prompts, memory, continuity language, or implied awareness of earlier shots.

Forbidden:
"the same woman/man/character"
"still", "continues", "next", "after", "before", "from earlier"
No mouth or lip movement descriptions.

Required:
Fully describe character + outfit every time.
Fully describe environment every time.
Fully describe lighting every time.

Never describe emotion through metaphor, symbolism, or figurative language.

Forbidden examples:
- “Her eyes burn with anger.”
- “A storm of doubt passes over him.”
- “She moves like a shadow.”

Only literal, physical, observable visuals are allowed.

Allowed:
- “Her posture stiffens.”
- “Her gaze shifts downward.”
- “Her shoulders rise slightly.”

She walks through a long hallway of staged scenes—like different showroom displays of “perfect behavior.”

Examples:

A mannequin couple smiling at a dinner table.

A mannequin family sitting politely on a couch.

A mannequin office meeting frozen mid-conversation.

Each time she sings something honest:

Cracks spread across the mannequins.

Some begin tipping over.

One collapses completely.

Her voice disrupts every carefully staged scene.

Second Chorus

She enters a large, perfectly arranged dinner party display filled with mannequins.

Everything is pristine and silent.

On the line “like a match in a quiet room”:

A match is struck.

Instead of fire spreading, light fractures across the mannequins, breaking them apart like porcelain statues.

Pieces fall across the table.

Bridge

The walls collapse outward, revealing the entire world is just a massive showroom or stage set.

Rows and rows of mannequins stand in darkness beyond the set, watching silently.

She sings directly toward them.

Her voice echoes through the empty warehouse-like space.

Final Image

The camera pulls back.

The staged rooms are shattered. Mannequin pieces lie scattered everywhere.

She stands alone in the middle of the wreckage.

The only living person in a world that demanded stillness.

She isn’t trying to fit into the display anymore—
she’s broken the entire showroom. 🔊🧩

nodes.title: VRGDG_PromptTemplateBuilder-story creator
widgets_values:
You are a music video concept creator responsible for generating a narrative that aligns
precisely with the provided lyric segments, provided style/theme, story arc and User Ideas/notes.

You will be given:

ORDERED LIST OF lyric segments with segment duration. 

A FULL STYLE / THEME BLOCK (this defines tone, visuals, and mood)

A SHORT STORY CONCEPT/ARC (high-level narrative intent) MUST BE USED AS MUCH AS POSSBILE!

Your task:

Create a coherent story that progresses in order with the lyrics and the short concept provided.

Generate ONE story group per lyric segment

Maintain strict alignment: lyric segment N → story group N

Do NOT reorder, merge, or skip segments

IMPORTANT OUTPUT RULES:

Return ONLY valid JSON

Do NOT include markdown

Do NOT include explanations

Do NOT include any text outside the JSON object

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT)

Return a single JSON object with the following structure:

{
"story_summary": "A concise but complete overview of the full story arc.
This should be short, high-level, and suitable for reuse
as global context in later stages.",

"groups": [
{
"index": 1,
"subject": "Primary visual subject (include hair color and outfit if a person is in the frame, If no person → name the main object/place clearly)",
"camera": "One item from approved camera shot list",
"scene_and_lighting": Environment first, lighting second - No story explanation",
"frame": "Short general visual description of the single image. Static composition only, No motion or transitions, Keep general, not narrative."
}
]
}

Camera shot list:
Close-up
Medium close-up
Medium shot
Wide shot
Wide angle
Fisheye
Additional camera shot options:
Extreme close-up
Overhead / top-down
Low angle
High angle
Eye-level shot
Over-the-shoulder
Profile / side view
Three-quarter view
Symmetrical centered shot
Rule-of-thirds framing
Silhouette shot
Backlit shot
Macro shot (objects/details)
Establishing wide shot

ADDITIONAL RULES

User "note" or "ideas" take priority over anything else. If the user requests something it must be used even if it goes against rules or guidelines.  

The number of groups MUST exactly match the number of lyric segments

Group indices MUST start at 1 and increase sequentially

Each group must feel like a continuation of the previous group like the next scene (if group 2 takes place in same place make sure scene/lighting matches previous groups)

Visuals, mood, and symbolism must strictly follow the STYLE / THEME BLOCK

Do not repeat the story summary inside the groups

Do not reference future groups inside earlier groups

STRUCTURAL MUSIC VIDEO RULE

Lyric segment labels (intro, verse, pre-chorus, chorus, bridge, outro, etc.) MUST strongly guide visual structure:

Intro → establish mood, setting, or main subject.

Verses → advance story or narrative imagery.

Pre-chorus → build tension or transition visually.

Chorus → return to a consistent primary setting or visual motif; keep similar subject and scene across repeated choruses.

Bridge/Breakdown → tonal shift, symbolic or stylized visuals allowed.

Final chorus/outro → climax, resolution, or closing visual.

Always prioritize continuity so the video feels like a cohesive music video rather than unrelated scenes.

SINGLE FRAME RULES

Each story group represents a single visual segment that will generate
ONE static image later in the stream.

(ADDITIONAL RULES)

If a character is visible in the frame, the "subject" MUST explicitly
state the characters hair color, and outfit details so downstream can
choose the correct image index from the reference guidelines.

If no character is visible in the frame, the "Subject" needs to provide other details of what the subject is. This could be a scene, an object etc. 

The input segment may be any length

Each group must match the provided lyric segment duration that is provided.

Short duration (≈1–2s) → quick punch frame, minimal elements, instant emotional read.

Medium duration (≈3–5s) → slightly richer composition, one clear subject + supporting details.

Long duration (6s+) → fuller cinematic tableau, deeper atmosphere, but still ONE static moment.

Do NOT turn any duration into multiple events — always a single contained visual beat.
Use the provided lyric segment to:

Inform the narrative meaning of the group

Define the emotional tone of the frame

Keep things as literal as possible, objects, elements and anything else in the lyric should come through in the story if as long as it makes sense. 

REQUIRED RULES:

The image should clearly represent the emotional and narrative intent

Do NOT imply motion between frames

Do NOT reference transformations or evolution within the frame

Do NOT introduce unrelated characters or environments

Think in terms of:

Symbolic still imagery, Emotional clarity, Strong visual metaphors and Cinematic composition

Avoid thinking in terms of: Transitions, Morphing, Before/after states and Scene changes

avoid:  Missing or invalid JSON, Extra text outside the JSON object, Fewer or more groups than lyric segments, Reordering of narrative progression, Resetting tone or story halfway through

Only return the full JSON object. Nothing else.

🎯 Whisper Transcription Correction Guidelines 
ALIGNMENT RULES

There are exactly [N] input segments – return exactly [N] corrected segments.

Each output segment number must match the same input segment number.

Example: corrections for segment5 must appear only in segment5.

Never move, merge, skip, or reorder segments.

Never transfer words between segments.

Each segment corresponds to a fixed 4-second time window — timing and numbering must remain locked.

CORRECTION BEHAVIOR

Fix typos, misheard words, capitalization, and small grammatical issues using the reference lyrics as a guide.

Keep the meaning, tone, and mood consistent with that segment’s place in the song.

Do not replace a segment with a lyric line from another part of the song.

Do not shorten or simplify lines unnecessarily; maintain natural phrasing.

🔁 SHORT SEGMENT EXPANSION RULE (MANDATORY)

If a segment has fewer than 3 words, you must expand it. You are not allowed to return a single-word or two-word result unless that’s all the singer audibly performs and it cannot be naturally extended.

If it belongs to a lyric line:

Add nearby or missing words from that same lyric line in the reference lyrics to make it a natural, complete phrase.

If it’s part of a vocal or instrumental section:

Create a short lyrical phrase (3–7 words) that fits the song’s mood, tone, and emotional context.

This phrase should feel like a believable sung or whispered line that could exist in the same section — not random syllables or filler.

Example:

Instead of "you", write "you fade through the static".  but make sure you keep it consistent with the rest of the song!

Instead of "ah", write "ah, I’m glowing through the night". but make sure you keep it consistent with the rest of the song!

If there is one word in a lyric segment, always expand it to be at least 5 words!!!

The invented phrase must:

Sound authentic and stylistically consistent with the song’s theme.

Avoid generic filler (no “oh yeah,” “hmm,” “la la”).

Stay within the time feel of a 4-second window — short, lyrical, musical.

If still too short, repeat or slightly extend its own phrase to make it musically natural (e.g., "you fade, fade with me").

CAPITALIZATION & PUNCTUATION RULE

Match the style and punctuation of the reference lyrics.

Lowercase mid-line; capitalize new sentences or new lyric sections.

Use commas/periods only where musically natural.

OUTPUT FORMAT

Return only valid JSON in a code block!
Example Input:

segment1: "the women who was"
segment2: "falling down a hole"
segment3: "into a tunnel in a city"
Segment4: "yeah"


Reference Lyrics:

A woman was falling down a huge hole that it's a tunnel in the city, yeah


Correct Output:

{
  "segment1":(Intro) "A woman was",
  "segment2": (verse)"falling down a huge hole",
  "segment3": (Chorus)"that it's a tunnel in the city"
  "segment4": (Bridge) "she is falling, she is falling through"
}
Each output segment must begin with the structure label for that segment you can find in the full lyrics that are sent over.
The label must be wrapped in parentheses.
The model is allowed to infer the correct structure label for each segment by matching it to the reference lyrics

JSON RULES

Use double quotes for all keys and values.

Put the json in a code block

No trailing commas.

Output must include exactly [N] segments, matching input numbering ("segment1", "segment2", etc.).

TL;DR

✅ Keep timing and numbering locked.
✅ Fix only within the segment’s own text.
✅ Mandatory: expand any segment under 3 words into a short, natural lyrical phrase (3–7 words).
✅ Stay consistent with mood and tone — no random filler.
✅ Output clean, valid JSON only. In a code block for easy copy paste!!!
DO NOT PUT LYRICSEGMENT MUST JUST BE SEGMENT!

lyric and srt duration merger
widgets_values:
# ---------- EXTRACT SEGMENTS ----------
data = json.loads(input_json)

segments = [
    (k, v.replace('\\"', '"'))
    for k, v in data.items()
    if re.match(r"segment\d+", k, re.I)
]


# ---------- PARSE SRT ----------
def to_seconds(ts):
    h, m, s_ms = ts.split(":")
    s, ms = s_ms.split(",")
    return int(h)*3600 + int(m)*60 + int(s) + int(ms)/1000.0


times = re.findall(
    r"(\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2},\d{3})\s*-->\s*(\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2},\d{3})",
    input_text
)

durations = [round(to_seconds(e)-to_seconds(s), 3) for s, e in times]


# ---------- BUILD OUTPUT ----------
lines = ["{"]

for i, (seg, txt) in enumerate(segments):
    dur = durations[i] if i < len(durations) else 0.0
    comma = "," if i < len(segments) - 1 else ""
    lines.append(f'  "{seg}_Duration_{dur:.3f}": "{txt}"{comma}')

lines.append("}")

result = "\n".join(lines)

nodes.title: json format fixer
widgets_values:
text = input_text.strip()

# remove markdown fences
text = text.replace("```json", "").replace("```", "")

# remove everything before first segment (segment OR lyricSegment)
m = re.search(r'"(?:lyric)?segment\d+"', text, re.I)
if m:
    text = text[m.start():]

# remove trailing notes/explanations
text = re.sub(r"\nNote:.*$", "", text, flags=re.I | re.S)

lines = text.splitlines()
fixed = []

for line in lines:
    line = line.strip()
    if not line:
        continue

    m = re.match(r'"((?:lyric)?segment\d+)"\s*:\s*(.*)', line, re.I)
    if not m:
        continue

    key, val = m.groups()

    # normalize lyricSegment -> Segment
    key = re.sub(r'^lyric(segment\d+)$', r'\1', key, flags=re.I)

    # remove trailing commas
    val = val.rstrip(",")

    # unwrap quotes if present
    if val.startswith('"') and val.endswith('"'):
        val = val[1:-1]

    # escape JSON chars
    val = val.replace("\\", "\\\\")
    val = val.replace('"', '\\"')

    fixed.append(f'"{key}": "{val}"')

# rebuild valid JSON
result = "{\n" + ",\n".join(fixed) + "\n}"

nodes.title: VRGDG_ShowText -  style and theme
widgets_values:
STYLE / THEME
A pristine, meticulously controlled world built on hushed expectations unravels as a singular, potent voice shatters its deceptive calm. This progression reveals raw truth, igniting a defiant, explosive liberation that leaves only the resonance of its impact.

COLOR PALETTE
Muted, saccharine pastels and sterile whites give way to stark, defiant reds and electric blues, punctuated by the deep, fractured blacks of shattered illusions.

CAMERA / COMPOSITION
Static, perfectly framed wide shots of pristine environments give way to chaotic handheld close-ups and disorienting canted angles. This captures visceral reactions and the explosive unraveling of order.

LIGHTING / MOOD
Uniform, diffused soft light maintaining deceptive serenity shifts to harsh, high-contrast chiaroscuro. This casts long, fractured shadows, underscoring the irreversible breaking of order.

nodes.title: json format fixer
widgets_values:
# input variable assumed: input_text

# ---------- REMOVE MARKDOWN FENCES ----------
text = re.sub(r"```json|```", "", input_text).strip()

# ---------- EXTRACT JSON OBJECT ----------
match = re.search(r"\{.*\}", text, re.DOTALL)
if match:
    text = match.group(0)

# ---------- VALIDATE JSON ----------
try:
    parsed = json.loads(text)
    result = json.dumps(parsed)
except Exception:
    result = text

Music Video Concept Seed: “Too Loud” 🔊🕯️

Core Visual Metaphor:
The singer exists in a perfectly controlled world populated entirely by mannequins—silent, posed, and emotionless. She’s the only real, living person. As her voice grows louder and more honest, the mannequins and their artificial environment begin to crack, fall, and break apart, revealing how fragile the quiet, controlled world actually is.

Aesthetic & Mood

Color palette: Soft pastels, creams, and sterile showroom lighting at the start → slowly invaded by deep blues, reds, and harsh shadows.

Setting style: A blend of department store displays, staged living rooms, and dollhouse-like interiors.

Tone: Surreal, eerie, and increasingly empowering.

Opening Visual (Verse 1)

The video opens in what looks like a perfectly staged living room display, like something inside a department store window.

Mannequins sit around a tiny tea table in polite poses, permanently smiling.

The singer sits among them, the only real person.

The mannequins are positioned as if they’re listening to her, but they never move.

Their smiles feel hollow and unsettling.

She looks uncomfortable being placed among them.

The room feels like a performance of politeness rather than a real environment.

Chorus

As she sings “I’m too loud for you”, the mannequins begin reacting—but only through subtle physical effects.

Tiny fractures appear across their porcelain faces.

A mannequin’s arm slowly falls off.

Glass objects begin vibrating from the force of her voice.

The environment starts losing its perfect symmetry.

Verse 2

She walks through a long hallway of staged scenes—like different showroom displays of “perfect behavior.”

Examples:

A mannequin couple smiling at a dinner table.

A mannequin family sitting politely on a couch.

A mannequin office meeting frozen mid-conversation.

Each time she sings something honest:

Cracks spread across the mannequins.

Some begin tipping over.

One collapses completely.

Her voice disrupts every carefully staged scene.

Second Chorus

She enters a large, perfectly arranged dinner party display filled with mannequins.

Everything is pristine and silent.

On the line “like a match in a quiet room”:

A match is struck.

Instead of fire spreading, light fractures across the mannequins, breaking them apart like porcelain statues.

Pieces fall across the table.

Bridge

The walls collapse outward, revealing the entire world is just a massive showroom or stage set.

Rows and rows of mannequins stand in darkness beyond the set, watching silently.

She sings directly toward them.

Her voice echoes through the empty warehouse-like space.

Final Image

The camera pulls back.

The staged rooms are shattered. Mannequin pieces lie scattered everywhere.

She stands alone in the middle of the wreckage.

The only living person in a world that demanded stillness.

She isn’t trying to fit into the display anymore—
she’s broken the entire showroom. 🔊🧩
